title: centranthera lepidota
intro: Centranthera lepidota, also known as the leopard balm, is a plant species that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the Himalayan region, and its leaves and flowers are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The leaves and flowers of Centranthera lepidota can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and valuable medicinal plant.
